title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Buys 24,869 BTC for $2 Billion, Now Holds 4% of Bitcoin Supply
summary: MicroStrategy has added 24,869 BTC to its holdings, bringing its total to over 4% of the entire Bitcoin supply. This purchase, worth $2 billion, highlights the company's continued bullish stance on Bitcoin.
body:
MicroStrategy, led by Michael Saylor, has acquired an additional 24,869 Bitcoin (BTC) for approximately $2 billion. This purchase increases the company's total Bitcoin holdings to over 4% of the 21 million BTC supply cap, making it one of the largest corporate holders of Bitcoin.

The latest acquisition brings MicroStrategy's total Bitcoin holdings to a staggering 840,000 BTC, valued at around $65 billion. This move underscores the company's long-term strategy of treating Bitcoin as a primary reserve asset, despite market volatility.

For everyday investors, this news underscores Bitcoin's growing acceptance as a legitimate asset class. MicroStrategy's bold moves may inspire other corporations to consider Bitcoin as a hedge against inflation and economic uncertainty.

Investors should watch for further corporate adoption of Bitcoin and any regulatory responses to such large-scale purchases. MicroStrategy's actions could set a precedent for how other companies view Bitcoin as a strategic asset.
twitterThread: MicroStrategy just added 24,869 BTC to its holdings, now owning over 4% of all Bitcoin. That's $2 billion in a single move. This isn't just a company buying Bitcoin—it's a statement.
MicroStrategy now holds 840,000 BTC, worth around $65 billion. That's a massive bet on Bitcoin's long-term value, even as prices fluctuate. Other corporations are watching closely.

If big companies start following MicroStrategy's lead, Bitcoin could see even more mainstream adoption. Keep an eye on corporate balance sheets for the next big move. standaloneTweet: MicroStrategy just bought $2 billion worth of Bitcoin, now holding over 4% of the total supply. This isn't just an investment—it's a vote of confidence in Bitcoin's future.
